response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile polyglot engineer with genuine systems-programming depth, but almost none of it is in the stack this role demands. His only professional C++ experience is roughly seven months at National Instruments over a decade ago; his recent work is TypeScript/Node web product engineering and DLang services. There is no evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL secure-development practice, MQTT, message brokers at the required scale in C++, or the OT protocols listed as advantageous, and nothing indicating readiness for the mentoring/architecture-leadership expectations of a Senior Manager C++ position.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec, albeit in DLang)
Strong software-craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and a track record of teaching and running workshops
Long, broad engineering career spanning backend, embedded-adjacent, DevOps, and event-driven platform work
concerns: Minimal professional C++/STL experience (~7 months, 2014-2015); core skill for the role is effectively stale
No industrial cybersecurity, SDL/IEC 62443, MQTT, or industrial-protocol (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) exposure
No demonstrated people-management or formal technical-leadership experience matching a Senior Manager scope

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent hands-on C++ experience
No industrial automation or IEC 62443 security background
